data.title: Starboard Q1 moves include halving Pfizer stake amid activist challenge, taking in Qorvo
data.description:
Hedge fund Starboard halved its stake in Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) in the first quarter, amid an activist challenge to the healthcare giant. It also slightly reduced its holding in Tinder-parent Match (MTCH), another company it has targeted. Among other moves, it took a new stake in Apple (AAPL) supplier Qorvo (NASDAQ:QRVO). 

Starboard disclosed the information in its latest 13F filing for the three months ended March 31, 2025, published on Thursday.

The filing showed Starboard's stake in Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) stood at 7.7M shares at the end of Q1, compared to 14.7M at the end of Q4 2024. That's a decrease of 49.7%. 

Starboard last October took a $1B stake in Pfizer (NYSE:PFE), reaching out to two former executives of the pharmaceutical firm, Ian Read and Frank D'Amelio, to help in its campaign. According to the hedge fund, Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) "significantly overpaid" for recent acquisitions and is responsible for wiping off more than $20B of its value.

Meanwhile, Starboard cut its position in Match (MTCH) - the owner of popular dating apps such as Tinder and Hinge - by 3.9% to 14.1M shares in Q1. The activist investor last year built a stake in Match (MTCH), pushing for a business turnaround or a potential sale. 

Starboard in Q1 took a new stake of 8.3M shares in Qorvo (NASDAQ:QRVO), and 1.7M shares in medical device maker BD (BDX).

It nearly doubled its position in software firm Autodesk (ADSK) to 2M shares in Q1, and upped its stake in Dow 30 component Salesforce (CRM) by 51.9% to 850K shares. 

More on 13F filings

  • Philippe Laffont's Coatue Management takes new take in Philip Morris, cuts position in Tesla, Nvidia in Q1 moves

  • Michael Burry's Scion lightens up on healthcare portfolio, in Q1 moves

data.title: Whale Rock exits Salesforce, Reddit; adds Impinj, Bloom Energy among Q1 moves
data.description:
Whale Rock Capital Management took new stakes in Impinj (PI), Bloom Energy (BE), and MongoDB, while closing out positions in Salesforce (CRM), Reddit (RDDT), and Atlassian (TEAM), among its Q1 trades, according to its 13F filing.

It added 1.23M shares of Impinj (PI) valued at $111.7M as of March 31, 2025, 3.95M shares of Bloom Energy (BE) ($77.7M), and ~413K shares of MongoDB (MDB) ($72.4M).

It exited Salesforce (CRM), Reddit (RDDT), Atlassian (TEAM), and Coupang (CPNG).

The firm also increased its stakes in Okta (OKTA) to 2.54M ($267.6M) from ~814K shares; Microsoft (MSFT) to 537K ($201.7M) from ~25K shares; and Fabrinet (FN) to ~1.29M shares ($254.4M) from ~840K shares.

It reduced its stakes in: Broadcom (AVGO) to ~1.44M shares ($240.4M) from 2.56M shares; Amazon (AMZN) to 2.52M shares ($480.1M) from 3.49M; and Toast (TOST) to ~1.40M ($46.4M) from 7.26M, according to the filing.

data.title: 3G Capital exits Uber & Broadcom, raises stakes in Meta, Amazon in Q1 moves
data.description:
Global investment firm and private partnership 3G Capital in the first quarter picked up a new stake in Intuit (NASDAQ:INTU) and Workday (NASDAQ:WDAY), increased its stakes in Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) and Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN), and exited Uber Technologies (NYSE:UBER), among other moves.

3G Capital disclosed the information in its latest 13F filing, which was published on Thursday.

The investment firm exited its stake in the ride-hailing platform Uber (NYSE:UBER) as well as Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO), selling 275k and 70k shares, respectively. Other exits include Affirm Holdings (AFRM), Sprout Social (SPT) and BILL Holdings (BILL).

Some new stakes in the quarter include 350k class A common shares in Mexican grocery retail store operator BBB Foods (TBBB), 100k series A common stocks in Klaviyo (KVYO), a stake of 15K shares in cloud application provider Workday (NASDAQ:WDAY), 15k shares in Monday.com (MNDY) and 10k shares in financial product and services provider Intuit (NASDAQ:INTU).

3G Capital piled into its Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) holdings to 125K in Q1 2025 from 65K in the previous quarter and increased its Meta (NASDAQ:META) stake by about 10% to 85K. The investment firm strengthened its stake in Analog Devices (ADI) by adding 65K shares to 200K in Q1.

The investment firm also increased its stakes in Carvana (CVNA), Coupang (CPNG), Atour Lifestyle (ATAT).

According to the filing, 3G Capital trimmed its position in companies including PDD Holdings (PDD), MercadoLibre (MELI), DraftKings (DKNG) and Goosehead Insurance (GSHD).

More on 13Fs

  • Seth Klarman's Baupost adds FIS, SGI stakes, dumps SNRE, HUM, among Q1 moves

  • Bridgewater Associates adds new Netflix, UAL stakes, exits 3M, Amgen, among other Q1 moves
  • Engaged Capital's Q1 moves include new stake in Yeti, GXO, exits PRA Group

data.title: U.S. considers fighter jet projects including F-22 upgrade, twin-engine F-55
data.description:
The United States is exploring plans for two new fighter jet initiatives: a modernized version of the F-22 Raptor, potentially called the "F-22 Super," and a fresh twin-engine platform known as the F-55, President Donald Trump said during a business forum in Doha on Thursday.

The event, attended by executives from Boeing (NYSE:BA) and GE Aerospace (NYSE:GE), came just after Trump announced a major commercial deal involving the purchase of 160 Boeing (NYSE:BA) airliners by Qatar, according to multiple press reports.

Trump described the F-55 as both an evolution of the F-35 and a distinct aircraft, hinting at a possible alternative proposed by Lockheed Martin (NYSE:LMT) following its recent loss to Boeing in a competition to replace the aging F-22 fleet.

In addition to touting the F-55, Trump highlighted the F-47 -- a next-generation air superiority platform that integrates manned aircraft with drone support. Boeing (BA) was recently selected to develop the F-47, part of the Pentagon’s broader shift toward sixth-generation fighter capabilities.

Trump signaled urgency in advancing both platforms. His comments followed Lockheed Martin’s (NYSE:LMT) recent statements about adapting the technologies from its unsuccessful F-47 proposal into a new “fifth-generation-plus” concept—potentially an enhanced F-35 variant. 

Lockheed (LMT), which is also working on long-delayed upgrades to the F-35’s software and cockpit systems, thanked Trump for supporting its legacy aircraft and expressed willingness to collaborate on future defense priorities.

While Boeing (BA) has taken the lead in next-gen fighter contracts, Lockheed remains (LMT) active in developing more affordable, high-performance alternatives.

data.title: Soroban Capital takes new stakes in Amazon, Western Digital, exits Lowe's, among other Q1 moves
data.description:
Soroban Capital, the hedge fund led by Eric Mandelblatt, opened new equity holdings in Amazon (AMZN), with 1.77M shares, Western Digital (WDC), with 151.1K shares, and Rexford Industrial Realty (REXR), with 5.85M shares, during Q1 2025 according to its latest 13F released Thursday.

On the other side of the fence, it closed its position in Lowe's Companies (LOW) and VeriSign (VRSN).

Its holdings were reduced in Ameren (AEE), to 1.13M shares in Q1 from 2.70M shares in Q4 2024, Mastercard (MA), to 922.2K from 981K, and CSX (CSX), to 6.92M from 17.0M.

Increased position sizes featured Microsoft (MSFT), to 4.11M from 3.44M, the filing showed.

data.title: Contango Ore outlines 2025 gold production guidance of 60,000 ounces while targeting debt reduction to $15M
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Contango Ore (CTGO) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Rick Van Nieuwenhuyse indicated, "things are going a little better than planned. I think the total amount of gold produced was more than planned, roughly 30% for Q1." He noted production outperformance and highlighted the campaign production process, clarifying that "there's always probably be a golden inventory that's been produced, but not sold necessarily."
  • CFO Mike Clark reported, "we recorded $19 million in income from operations, which includes $22.3 million in equity income from the Peak Gold JV, which is our 30% ownership in Manh Choh. We recorded a net loss of $22.5 million for the quarter, which includes an unrealized loss of $40.5 million related to the hedge contracts." Clark also stated, "Our 2025 guidance remains at 60,000 ounces of gold with an ASIC of about $1,625 as we do expect, the ASIC will increase in later quarters due to sustaining capital going up related to replacing tractors on the ore haul route as well as a $5.7 million exploration drill program." He further highlighted a cash position of $35 million and subsequent increases in marketable securities.
  • Van Nieuwenhuyse commented on strategic progress at Johnson Tract, noting that permitting for the underground access tunnel is the next milestone, and the process is expected to take about a year.

Outlook

  • Clark reaffirmed, "Our 2025 guidance remains at 60,000 ounces of gold with an ASIC of about $1,625." He added that ASIC is expected to increase in later quarters due to capital expenditures and an exploration drill program. Management expects to finish the year with the facility balance at about $15 million. Clark stated, "the facility will finish the year around $15 million just under."
  • Van Nieuwenhuyse stated, "We still hope to beat that but we still think that's a reasonable estimate" in reference to all-in sustaining costs.

Financial Results

  • Clark detailed that the quarter included $22.3 million in equity income from the Peak Gold JV and a net loss of $22.5 million, which factored in an unrealized loss of $40.5 million from hedge contracts. The company sold just over 17,000 ounces of gold, with another 3,800 ounces in recoverable inventory. Cash costs were $1,334 per ounce gold sold, and ASIC was $1,374 per ounce of gold sold for Q1. The balance sheet showed $35 million in cash and a reduction in the facility balance to $30 million as of the call date, including $13.8 million and $8.2 million in principal repayments during and subsequent to the quarter, respectively.
  • Clark explained the carry trade and hedge delivery schedule, noting that while the hedge balance started and ended the quarter at 86,000 ounces, the effective balance declined to below 75,000 ounces after considering carry trades, and as of the call, stood at approximately 71,000 ounces.

Q&A

  • Romeo Maione, 6ix Inc: Asked about the carry trade and hedge schedule. Clark answered that the company has moved toward a carry trade approach, allowing gold to be sold at spot price as shipments occur: "it's a better cash management tool for us, with a relatively low cost."
  • Maione: Asked about Onyx Gold Corp shares. Clark explained that Contango inherited 5 million Onyx shares via the HighGold acquisition and as of the call, they are worth above CAD 5 million.
  • Maione: Inquired about the recently dismissed lawsuit. Van Nieuwenhuyse stated, "it's a good thing for the project and for the Manh Choh project. But it's also I think a good thing for mining in general."
  • Maione: Asked about the reasons for the lower Q1 ASIC. Clark attributed it to less sustaining capital in Q1 and higher gold production; Van Nieuwenhuyse described operational improvements.
  • Maione: Questioned adjustments to hedging strategy. Clark responded, "just continuing to deliver into these hedges get ahead of them as much as we can... keep that 70-30 ratio as we deliver this year."
  • Maione: Asked about Johnson Tract development and permitting. Van Nieuwenhuyse explained the permitting would take about a year, focusing on road access and minimal environmental impact.
  • Maione: Asked about debt priorities. Van Nieuwenhuyse said the main focus remains paying down debt and delivering into hedges, with discretionary drilling at Lucky Shot dependent on cash position.
  • Maione: Asked about drilling plans. Van Nieuwenhuyse indicated Lucky Shot is the likely next site, with a Manh Choh joint venture program focusing on pit-adjacent targets.
  • Maione: Asked about short interest. Van Nieuwenhuyse commented, "I think the shorts are in trouble and I think today is a good demonstration of that."
  • Maione: Asked about confidence in extending Manh Choh mine life. Van Nieuwenhuyse stated, "I think we'll continue to see very strong cash flows out of Manh Choh for the next four years."
  • Maione: Asked about buyback and dividend plans. Van Nieuwenhuyse stated, "the main order business is paying off the debt and delivering the hedges."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed a positive tone, congratulating management on beating quarterly guidance and operational improvements, while seeking clarification on hedging, cash priorities, and project timelines.
  • Management conveyed a confident and slightly optimistic tone, frequently referencing operational outperformance, improved cash management, and strategic focus on debt reduction and project advancement. Clark and Van Nieuwenhuyse consistently emphasized prudent financial decision-making and risk management.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management displayed increased confidence, with management highlighting stronger-than-anticipated operating results and progress on debt reduction.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Current quarter guidance remains at 60,000 ounces of gold with ASIC unchanged at $1,625, consistent with the previous quarter. However, management noted Q1 gold production exceeded plan by roughly 30% and Q1 ASIC was significantly below annual guidance, due to lower sustaining capital and operational improvements.
  • Previous quarter discussions focused on ramping up production and aligning debt/hedge repayments to the revised mine plan, while the current quarter emphasized execution, improved cost structure, and a positive outlook for the remainder of the year.
  • Analyst focus shifted from concerns over debt and hedging to operational execution and strategic capital allocation, as well as exploration priorities.
  • Management sentiment was more upbeat and confident in the current quarter, citing positive operational trends and a resolved legal challenge.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited increasing ASIC in later quarters due to higher sustaining capital and a $5.7 million exploration drill program at Manh Choh.
  • The company continues to manage gold price volatility and the impact on hedge liabilities, with Clark stating, "if gold stayed where it is, our hedge liability should be cut in half by the end of the year."
  • The bridge weight restriction remains an operational constraint, with planned updates now scheduled for 2026.
  • There are still lockups on the Onyx Gold Corp shares, potentially limiting liquidity, though management noted the asset's value.
  • Analyst questions highlighted ongoing concerns about short interest and mine life extension.

Final Takeaway

Contango Ore management emphasized a strong start to 2025, delivering higher-than-planned gold production, maintaining robust cash flow, and continuing to reduce debt. Guidance for full-year gold output and costs remains unchanged, but management noted that future quarters will see higher all-in sustaining costs as capital expenditures ramp up. Operational improvements and the resolution of a significant legal challenge support a positive near-term outlook, while strategic focus remains on paying down debt, managing hedge obligations, and advancing key projects like Johnson Tract. The company is confident in its ability to sustain production, manage risks, and capitalize on strong gold prices for the remainder of the year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Zealand CEO: Oral weight loss meds are not the future for treating obesity
data.description:
Although some individuals might find taking a medicine as a pill more palatable than an injection, when it comes to future weight loss drugs, an oral treatment won't dethrone injectable or subcutaneous treatments for weight loss, according to Zealand Pharma CEO Adam Steensberg.

The reason, the exec said, is that relatively few individuals have such a deep fear of needles that they wouldn't take a medicine with such important benefits and oral medicines may have more side effects.

Zealand is developing several treatments for obesity in mid-to-late stage development, all of which are given via injection. The Danish company has partnerships with Boehringer Ingelheim and inked its latest deal -- worth $5.3B -- with Roche (OTCQX:RHHBY) in March.

Speaking via video at the FT Live Pharma and Biotech Summit in New York City, Steensberg noted that the next-generation of obesity drugs need to do a better job of also addressing co-morbidities, as well as finding doses that allow an individual to maintain weight loss while minimizing side effects.

He added that going forward, these are the most important characteristics weight loss treatments should possess, rather than maximizing the amount of weight loss.

Evidation Health Chief Commercial Officer Phil Johnson agreed, noting that data from 10,000 GLP-1 users he examined found that they were more concerned with side effects than delivery mechanism.

data.title: Activist hedge fund ValueAct boosts stakes in Amazon, Meta in Q1 moves
data.description:
Activist hedge fund ValueAct Holdings boosted its stakes in Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) and Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META), among its first quarter moves.

ValueAct disclosed the information in its latest 13F filing, which was published on Thursday.

During the quarter, the fund increased its position on Amazon to 0.56 million from 0.54 million, while it piled class A shares of Meta to 1.01 million from 0.21 million. Other than that, ValueAct also raised its stakes in Visa (V).

According to the filing, the fund took a new stake of 6.73 million class A shares of Rocket Companies (RKT) and 3.42 million class A shares of Nu Holdings (NU).

Meanwhile, it reduced its position in Roblox (RBLX), Toast (TOST), Live Nation Entertainment (LYV) and Expedia Group (EXPE).

data.title: Gold rises as tame economic data boosts chance for Fed rate cuts
data.description:
Gold futures gained Thursday after U.S. economic data appeared to support the case for the Federal Reserve to continue cutting interest rates this year.

The surprise pullback in the U.S.-China trade conflict sparked a risk-on rally in the market to start the week, sapping gold's safe-haven appeal and driving investors to take profits, but focus on U.S. inflation data showed U.S. producer prices unexpectedly fell in April by the most in five years, while retail sales growth slowed, after a report earlier this week showed consumer prices rose less than expected in April.

Analysts said the data creates more room for the Fed to cut rates, with more dovish expectations building in the market; lower rates help increase the attractiveness of bullion, which is a non-interest bearing asset.

Russian President Putin's failure to show up for peace talks in Turkey dims expectations of progress towards a Ukraine peace deal, which may have helped to underpin gold prices.

The gold market appeared to ignore President Trump's comments that the U.S. was closer to an agreement that would curb Iran's nuclear program.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for May delivery closed +1.2% to $3,220.70/oz, its largest one-day dollar and percentage gain since May 6, and front-month May silver (XAGUSD:CUR) ended +0.8% to $32.481/oz; YTD, gold has gained 22.5% and silver has climbed 12.2%.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Michael Burry's Scion lightens up on healthcare portfolio, in Q1 moves
data.description:
Scion Asset Management, founded by Michael Burry of "Big Short" fame, exited five stakes in healthcare-related companies in Q1 2025, among other moves, according to the firm's 13F filing.

The firm closed out positions in American Coastal Insurance (ACIC), Bruker (BRKR), HCA Healthcare (HCA), Magnera (MAGN), Molina Healthcare (MOH), and Oscar Healthcare (OSCER), as well as apparel makers Canada Goose (GOOS) and VF Corp. (VFC).

It increased its stake in Estee Lauder (EL) to 200K shares ($13.2M) from 100K shares, according to the filing.
